identify_pain.narrative: Cadence articulated three operational pain points: (1) manual, time-consuming search across their contract repository in iManage, (2) regulatory compliance complexity requiring centralized risk visibility, and (3) invoice processing inefficiency with vendor/PO matching errors and non-PO invoice handling gaps. The POC wrap-up 20 May confirmed 'consensus that iRisk POC met needs' for contract risk visibility. Pain owners are distributed across legal (contracts), finance (invoicing), and IT (architecture/integration), but no single pain owner has been identified as champion or EB. The pain is real and acknowledged; the missing pieces are quantified impact and executive-level sponsorship to drive the buy.

paper_process.narrative: No contracting discussion has occurred. MSA/SOW scope, legal review timeline, InfoSec requirements, procurement channel, and implementation scoping (migration + Coupa integration) are all undefined. The migration scoping itself is incomplete: iManage extraction methodology, migration volume/cost limits, and Coupa middleware scope were flagged as open items on 21 Apr and 28 Apr calls and remain unresolved 10+ weeks later, blocking SOW finalization. Without a contracting timeline or migration proposal the deal cannot progress to negotiation, and the 31 Jul close date is unreachable. The immediate move is to finalize the migration proposal and present a contracting roadmap to the buyer in the next 14 days.

flags: EXECUTIVE ENGAGEMENT REQUIRED — No C-level or VP-level Cadence participant has appeared on any call or in contact roles despite a $65k CLM+iRisk deal with cross-functional scope (procurement, legal, IT). Peter Hughes (Senior Category Manager) and Devesh Chadha (Director Procurement COE) are engaged influencers but neither is the Economic Buyer. The wrap-up (20 May) confirmed 'consensus-driven decision and enterprise architecture review' are gates, meaning exec-level approval is required, but the EB and approval chain are unmapped. An executive connect (Alexa Bradley to the relevant stakeholder, likely the VP Global Strategic Sourcing from account contacts) is the single highest-leverage move to: map the EB and decision committee, understand architecture review timeline and pass/fail criteria, create urgency for a near-term decision, and convert technical POC success into commercial momentum. This is a $65k enterprise deal 35 days from close with zero exec engagement—executive sponsorship is the missing lever to restart the stalled deal.

COMPETITIVE BLIND SPOT — Zero competitive intelligence across all sources (Salesforce fields, 21 Avoma touchpoints, Next Step notes, Tasks). Next Step (26 May) references 'scoring criteria' implying multi-vendor RFP but Cadence has not disclosed shortlist, criteria, or how Zycus ranks. Without knowing the alternatives Zycus cannot differentiate, cannot craft a competitive wedge, and cannot pressure-test win probability. This is either a sole-source eval (positive, but unconfirmed) or Cadence is running parallel vendor POCs without cross-disclosure (risk of surprise loss to undisclosed finalist or incumbent). Immediate move: ask Peter Hughes or Devesh directly 'Who else are you evaluating for CLM and iRisk, and how do we compare on your scorecard?' Obtaining the RFP criteria and competitive landscape is gate to confirm positioning and tailor the close strategy.

CHAMPION COOLING AND 37-DAY SILENCE — Peter Hughes (13 calls, POC wrap-up lead, articulated business needs) was the most engaged Cadence stakeholder but has gone silent for 37 days since 20 May wrap-up. Rep emailed Shreya (not Peter) 26 May for tracking update with no logged reply in 31 days. This signals either: (1) Peter handed coordination to Shreya post-POC and is awaiting internal decision (passive advocate, not actively driving), (2) Peter's influence has limits and he cannot move the deal without EB/exec sponsorship (champion strength 'developing' not 'strong'), or (3) the deal has deprioritized internally and the champion has cooled (risk of no-decision or loss to status quo). No Cadence participant has introduced Zycus to the EB, pushed back on a competitor, or created urgency. Immediate move: re-engage Peter directly (not Shreya) to confirm his continued advocacy, understand internal decision status and architecture review timeline, and secure an introduction to the EB or decision committee. A cooling champion 35 days from close with no EB access is a leading indicator of deal loss—reactivating Peter and escalating to exec sponsorship are the critical path.

Close date 31 Jul is 35 days out but deal has been silent for 37 days since POC wrap-up (20 May); no stage progression from the relevant stakeholder in 59 days; rep emailed Shreya 26 May with no reply in 31 days; architecture review timeline unknown; at current pace zero movement toward close, date is indefensible

EXECUTIVE ENGAGEMENT REQUIRED — No C-level or VP-level Cadence participant has appeared on any call or in contact roles despite a $65k CLM+iRisk deal with cross-functional scope (procurement, legal, IT). Peter Hughes (Senior Category Manager) and Devesh Chadha (Director Procurement COE) are engaged influencers but neither is the Economic Buyer. The wrap-up (20 May) confirmed 'consensus-driven decision and enterprise architecture review' are gates, meaning exec-level approval is required, but the EB and approval chain are unmapped. An executive connect (Alexa Bradley to the relevant stakeholder, likely the VP Global Strategic Sourcing from account contacts) is the single highest-leverage move to: map the EB and decision committee, understand architecture review timeline and pass/fail criteria, create urgency for a near-term decision, and convert technical POC success into commercial momentum. This is a $65k enterprise deal 35 days from close with zero exec engagement—executive sponsorship is the missing lever to restart the stalled deal.

COMPETITIVE BLIND SPOT — Zero competitive intelligence across all sources (Salesforce fields, 21 Avoma touchpoints, Next Step notes, Tasks). Next Step (26 May) references 'scoring criteria' implying multi-vendor RFP but Cadence has not disclosed shortlist, criteria, or how Zycus ranks. Without knowing the alternatives Zycus cannot differentiate, cannot craft a competitive wedge, and cannot pressure-test win probability. This is either a sole-source eval (positive, but unconfirmed) or Cadence is running parallel vendor POCs without cross-disclosure (risk of surprise loss to undisclosed finalist or incumbent). Immediate move: ask Peter Hughes or Devesh directly 'Who else are you evaluating for CLM and iRisk, and how do we compare on your scorecard?' Obtaining the RFP criteria and competitive landscape is gate to confirm positioning and tailor the close strategy.

CHAMPION COOLING AND 37-DAY SILENCE — Peter Hughes (13 calls, POC wrap-up lead, articulated business needs) was the most engaged Cadence stakeholder but has gone silent for 37 days since 20 May wrap-up. Rep emailed Shreya (not Peter) 26 May for tracking update with no logged reply in 31 days. This signals either: (1) Peter handed coordination to Shreya post-POC and is awaiting internal decision (passive advocate, not actively driving), (2) Peter's influence has limits and he cannot move the deal without EB/exec sponsorship (champion strength 'developing' not 'strong'), or (3) the deal has deprioritized internally and the champion has cooled (risk of no-decision or loss to status quo). No Cadence participant has introduced Zycus to the EB, pushed back on a competitor, or created urgency. Immediate move: re-engage Peter directly (not Shreya) to confirm his continued advocacy, understand internal decision status and architecture review timeline, and secure an introduction to the EB or decision committee. A cooling champion 35 days from close with no EB access is a leading indicator of deal loss—reactivating Peter and escalating to exec sponsorship are the critical path.
